campgrounds.name: Frenchman Campground (Frenchman Lake)
campgrounds.address: Forest Road 178, Chilcoot, CA 96105 (Plumas National Forest)
campgrounds.pet_notes: Standard USFS rules — leashed at all times
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Checked live Recreation.gov availability on 2026-07-07: 3 sites in the same FREN loop (013, 014, 017) are actually open for all of Aug 6-7-8 right now. Posted max vehicle lengths: site 013 = 40ft, site 014 = 35ft, site 017 = 55ft — enough spread to fit the motorhome, travel trailer, and cargo trailer between them, same loop, not touching. Book soon, these are individual sites and could go any time: site 013 (recreation.gov/camping/campsites/61141), site 014 (/campsites/61274), site 017 (/campsites/61410).
campgrounds.notes: Top pick for affordable + no hookups: $38/night, vault toilets, pine-covered hillside over a real lake (21 miles of shoreline, boating/fishing/swimming). No potable water on site in some years — bring your own to be safe. High desert/pine transition zone rather than deep forest, but sites themselves are described as wooded. This is the only campground in the whole list with confirmed real availability for your exact dates as of the check date.

campgrounds.name: Prosser Family Campground (Prosser Reservoir)
campgrounds.address: Prosser Dam Rd, Truckee, CA 96161 (Tahoe National Forest)
campgrounds.pet_notes: Standard USFS rules — leashed at all times
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Checked live Recreation.gov availability on 2026-07-07: plenty of sites open individually on Aug 6 (19) and Aug 7 (5), but zero sites are free for all of Aug 6-7-8 at once — Aug 8 (a Saturday) is fully booked across all 29 sites. Not bookable as a single 3-night stay right now; worth watching for cancellations or asking Recreation.gov support directly, or consider it if your dates could shift a day earlier.
campgrounds.notes: Very affordable ($20/night + $5/extra vehicle) and sites accommodate up to 60ft, on a forested reservoir 10 minutes from Truckee. Good backup to Frenchman if those 3 sites get booked, but the Saturday-night gap is a real obstacle for your exact dates as of the check date.

campgrounds.name: Village Camp Truckee (formerly Coachland RV Park)
campgrounds.pet_notes: Pets welcome; on-site off-leash dog park
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Sites don't need to touch — same loop/section is fine. No stated RV size restrictions, a safe bet for the 35ft motorhome. Private reservation system (not recreation.gov), so inventory isn't subject to the 6-month government booking rush.
campgrounds.notes: Pricier full-hookup option — keep in the list as a fallback if the budget dry-camping spots don't pan out, but not the primary pick anymore given the preference for no hookups and lower cost.

campgrounds.name: Plumas Pines Resort
campgrounds.pet_notes: Not confirmed — call to verify pet policy
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Sites don't need to touch — 63 sites gives real flexibility. $54-150/night depending on site — pricier than the dry-camping options.
campgrounds.notes: Mixed conifer forest on Lake Almanor, 40ft max fits both big rigs. Kept as a fallback — full hookups push the price above what you're looking for now.

campgrounds.name: Zephyr Cove Resort RV Park & Campground
campgrounds.pet_notes: Pet-friendly; dogs welcome on the restaurant deck
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Checked live Recreation.gov availability on 2026-07-07: zero RV-electric sites (of 93) show all three nights of Aug 6-7-8 open at once. Also the priciest option in the list.
campgrounds.notes: Nicest setting of the group but both sold out for these dates and the most expensive — no longer the top pick given the preference for affordable, no-hookup camping.

campgrounds.name: Gold Ranch Casino & RV Resort
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Sites don't need to touch — same section is fine. Call and ask for 3 sites near each other.
campgrounds.notes: River canyon setting, more valley/foothill than forested. Last-resort fallback — closest option (~12 miles) but full hookups and casino-resort pricing, not what you're after now.

campgrounds.name: River West Resort
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Small, established riverfront park close to downtown — call ahead since availability is tighter than the bigger resorts.
campgrounds.notes: Urban/valley setting, not forested, full hookup pricing. Only relevant as a last-minute emergency fallback.
